description: Add a server self-update notification and one-click update flow to the BrowserToolsCard in the side panel. When a newer version of @opentabs-dev/cli is available on npm AND the server is running from a production npm install (not from source), show a yellow update dot on the browser tools card icon and an 'Update to vX.Y.Z' menu item in BrowserToolsMenu. Clicking it triggers a 'phoenix restart': the server installs the new version via npm, spawns a new background server process, and exits. The extension reconnects automatically. Extension reload is handled automatically by the existing extensionHash mismatch detection.

userStories.title: Add serverUpdate field to ServerState and ConfigStateResult types
userStories.description: Add an optional serverUpdate field to ServerState in state.ts and to ConfigStateResult in shared/src/index.ts. This is purely a type and data-model addition — no logic changes.
acceptanceCriteria: ServerState has a field serverUpdate?: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string }
ConfigStateResult has a field serverUpdate?: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string }
createState() initializes serverUpdate to undefined (omitted by default since it is optional)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts (add serverUpdate field to ServerState interface and createState())
- platform/shared/src/index.ts (add serverUpdate field to ConfigStateResult)

Context:
ServerState is defined at platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts:277. It already has `outdatedPlugins: OutdatedPlugin[]` at line 296. The new field is analogous but for the server itself (not per-plugin).

ConfigStateResult is defined at platform/shared/src/index.ts:382. It already has `serverVersion?: string` and `serverSourcePath?: string`. The new field goes alongside these.

Changes:
In ServerState interface (state.ts:296, after `outdatedPlugins` field), add:
  /** Available CLI update detected by version check. Undefined when up-to-date or in dev mode. */
  serverUpdate?: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string };

In createState() (state.ts:373), no explicit initialization needed — optional fields default to undefined.

In ConfigStateResult (shared/src/index.ts:382), after `extensionHash?: string`, add:
  /** Available CLI update detected by the server. Omitted when up-to-date or running from source. */
  serverUpdate?: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string };

Constraints:
- Do NOT add serverUpdate to OutdatedPlugin or ConfigStatePlugin — those are per-plugin types
- Do NOT change STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION — only bump that when changing the type of an existing field, not when adding an optional field
- Do NOT touch any other interfaces or files

userStories.title: Add CLI version check, populate serverUpdate in payload builder, prune on reload
userStories.description: Add a checkServerUpdate() function to version-check.ts that queries the npm registry for @opentabs-dev/cli and stores results in state.serverUpdate. Only runs when the server is running from a production npm install (serverSourcePath contains 'node_modules'), not from source. Include state.serverUpdate in buildConfigStatePayload() and prune it in pruneStaleState() when the installed version matches latestVersion.
acceptanceCriteria: checkServerUpdate() function exists in version-check.ts and is exported
checkServerUpdate() sets state.serverUpdate when a newer @opentabs-dev/cli version exists
checkServerUpdate() does NOT set state.serverUpdate when the resolved source path does not contain 'node_modules' (running from source)
checkServerUpdate() clears state.serverUpdate to undefined when the CLI is already up-to-date
buildConfigStatePayload() includes serverUpdate from state when present, omits it when undefined
pruneStaleState() clears state.serverUpdate to undefined when state.serverUpdate.latestVersion matches the currently installed server version
runVersionCheck() calls checkServerUpdate(state) alongside checkForUpdates(state)

userStories.title: Add server.selfUpdate JSON-RPC handler with phoenix restart
userStories.description: Add a handleServerSelfUpdate handler that: runs npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli@<latestVersion>, prints a foreground-mode message to stdout if applicable, spawns opentabs start --background as a detached child, then exits. Register it in handleExtensionMessage for method 'server.selfUpdate'.
acceptanceCriteria: handleExtensionMessage dispatches 'server.selfUpdate' to handleServerSelfUpdate
handleServerSelfUpdate returns an error response if state.serverUpdate is undefined
handleServerSelfUpdate returns an error response when called on a dev install (not production)
handleServerSelfUpdate runs npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli@<latestVersion> before spawning
handleServerSelfUpdate spawns opentabs start --background --port <port> as a detached process with stdio ignored before exiting
When process.stdout.isTTY is true, handleServerSelfUpdate prints 'Updated to v<version>. Server restarted in background mode.' to stdout before exiting
process.exit(0) is called after spawning the new process

userStories.title: Add dev-only test HTTP endpoints for E2E testing the server update UI
userStories.description: Add POST /__test/set-server-update and POST /__test/simulate-server-update endpoints to http-routes.ts for E2E test injection, following the same pattern as /__test/set-outdated and /__test/simulate-update.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /__test/set-server-update returns 404 when isDev() is false
POST /__test/set-server-update sets state.serverUpdate from the request body and broadcasts plugins.changed
POST /__test/set-server-update accepts null to clear state.serverUpdate
POST /__test/simulate-server-update returns 404 when isDev() is false
POST /__test/simulate-server-update clears state.serverUpdate and broadcasts plugins.changed

userStories.title: Add serverUpdate through the extension pipeline to the side panel bridge
userStories.description: Propagate serverUpdate from the plugins.changed WebSocket payload through the extension's internal pipeline: server-state-cache.ts → message-router.ts extraction → extension-messages.ts new bg:selfUpdateServer message type → background-message-handlers.ts new handler → bridge.ts exposure and selfUpdateServer() function.
acceptanceCriteria: ServerStateCache interface has serverUpdate?: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string }
EMPTY_CACHE initializes serverUpdate to undefined
loadServerStateCacheFromSession restores serverUpdate with runtime type validation
message-router.ts extracts serverUpdate from plugins.changed/sync.full and passes it to updateServerStateCache
BgSelfUpdateServerMessage type exists in extension-messages.ts with type 'bg:selfUpdateServer'
BgSelfUpdateServerMessage is included in the InternalMessage union
bg:selfUpdateServer handler in background-message-handlers.ts calls sendServerRequest('server.selfUpdate', {})
FullStateResult in bridge.ts includes serverUpdate?: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string }
selfUpdateServer() function is exported from bridge.ts and sends { type: 'bg:selfUpdateServer' }

userStories.title: Add update dot and menu item to BrowserToolsCard and BrowserToolsMenu
userStories.description: Add the serverUpdate indicator to BrowserToolsCard (yellow dot on the icon) and a clickable 'Update to vX.Y.Z' first menu item to BrowserToolsMenu. Wire the click to selfUpdateServer() from bridge.ts. Show a loading spinner during the update and an error alert on failure. Connect the props from App.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolsCard accepts a serverUpdate prop of type { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string } | undefined
When serverUpdate is defined, a yellow dot (bg-primary, rounded-full, 2x2, positioned absolute -top-0.5 -right-0.5) is visible on the plugin icon area of BrowserToolsCard
When serverUpdate is undefined, no yellow dot is visible
BrowserToolsMenu renders 'Update to vX.Y.Z' as the first menu item when serverUpdate is defined
When serverUpdate is undefined, no Update menu item is rendered
Clicking the Update menu item calls selfUpdateServer() and shows a loading spinner in the menu item
If selfUpdateServer() rejects, an error alert is shown in BrowserToolsCard (same pattern as toggle errors)
App.tsx passes serverUpdate from the state cache to BrowserToolsCard
The BrowserToolsCard stores serverUpdate from getFullState() and updates it when plugins.changed is received

userStories.title: E2E tests for server self-update notification and phoenix restart flow
userStories.description: Write a comprehensive E2E test file e2e/side-panel-server-update.e2e.ts testing the full server self-update flow including: update indicator visibility, menu item content, click behavior, server disconnect/reconnect after update, extension reload dialog on hash change, double-click protection, and failure handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: update dot and menu item appear when serverUpdate is injected via POST /__test/set-server-update
Test: update dot and menu item do NOT appear when no serverUpdate is set (covers dev mode — the test server never has serverUpdate unless injected)
Test: update dot disappears after POST /__test/simulate-server-update clears the indicator
Test: clicking 'Update to vX.Y.Z' sends the server.selfUpdate request (observable via server log or request interception)
Test: after the server restarts (kill + restart in test), the extension reconnects and the update dot is absent
Test: when extensionHash changes between server restarts, ExtensionUpdateDialog appears after reconnect
Test: double-clicking Update only triggers one update request
Test: when server.selfUpdate fails (server returns error), an error alert appears in the side panel
All tests clean up their server, context, and temp dirs in finally blocks
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-server-update.e2e.ts (new file)

Context:
Follow the exact test structure from e2e/side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts. Key patterns to copy:
- Test fixture setup: startMcpServer, launchExtensionContext, openSidePanel
- Cleanup pattern: try/finally with context.close(), server.kill(), cleanupTestConfigDir(), rmSync(cleanupDir)
- Use waitForExtensionConnected(server) before any UI interactions
- Use expect.poll() for assertions that require server-side state propagation (not plain await expect())

Helper functions to add at top of file:

  const setServerUpdate = async (port: number, secret: string, serverUpdate: { latestVersion: string; updateCommand: string } | null): Promise<void> => {
    const res = await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/__test/set-server-update`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` },
      body: JSON.stringify({ serverUpdate }),
    });
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`setServerUpdate failed: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
  };

  const simulateServerUpdate = async (port: number, secret: string): Promise<void> => {
    const res = await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/__test/simulate-server-update`, {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', Authorization: `Bearer ${secret}` },
      body: JSON.stringify({}),
    });
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`simulateServerUpdate failed: ${res.status} ${await res.text()}`);
  };

Locators:
  - Browser tools menu button: sidePanelPage.locator('[aria-label="Browser tools options"]')
  - Update dot: browserMenuButton.locator('div.rounded-full')
  - Update menu item: sidePanelPage.locator('[role="menuitem"]', { hasText: /^Update to v/ })
  - Error alert: sidePanelPage.locator('[role="alert"]')
  - ExtensionUpdateDialog reload button: sidePanelPage.locator('button', { hasText: 'Reload' })

Test 1 — indicator appears and disappears:
  - Open side panel, verify no update dot initially
  - POST /__test/set-server-update { latestVersion: '99.0.0', updateCommand: 'npm install -g @opentabs-dev/cli' }
  - Verify update dot becomes visible (timeout 10_000)
  - Open menu, verify 'Update to v99.0.0' item is visible
  - POST /__test/simulate-server-update (clears indicator)
  - Verify update dot disappears (timeout 10_000)
  - Verify 'Update to v...' menu item gone

Test 2 — no dot without injection:
  - Open side panel, wait for it to load
  - Verify no update dot visible on browser tools card
  - Verify no 'Update to v...' menu item in browser tools menu

Test 3 — click Update sends request and handles phoenix restart:
  - Inject serverUpdate via test endpoint
  - Wait for dot to appear
  - Click 'Update to v99.0.0' menu item
  - The server.selfUpdate handler (in dev mode) will run npm install (which will fail since @opentabs-dev/cli@99.0.0 doesn't exist) and return an error
  - Verify error alert appears in the side panel with a message
  NOTE: We cannot test the actual phoenix restart (server exits) in unit-style E2E because it would kill the test's server handle. Instead, test the failure path to verify the UI handles errors correctly. The disconnect/reconnect path is covered by Test 4.

Test 4 — server disconnect and reconnect clears indicator:
  - Inject serverUpdate
  - Verify dot visible
  - Kill the server (server.kill())
  - Verify side panel shows disconnected state
  - Start new server on same configDir (startMcpServer(configDir, true, { port: server.port }) — check fixtures.ts for the port-override API; if not available, use any available port)
  - Wait for reconnect (waitForExtensionConnected)
  - Verify no update dot (new server has no serverUpdate)
  NOTE: This tests that reconnect clears the update indicator without needing to actually trigger the update flow.

Test 5 — extension reload dialog after hash change:
  - Kill server and restart with a different extensionHash (use the test endpoint POST /__test/set-server-update OR inject a different extensionHash by starting the server with a modified extension dir)
  - Actually: the extensionHash mismatch is already tested in the extension reconnect tests. Include a comment noting this and skip a duplicate test.

Test 6 — double-click protection:
  - Inject serverUpdate
  - Click Update menu item
  - Immediately try to click Update again (same pattern as plugin update double-click test, lines 323-400 of side-panel-plugin-update.e2e.ts)
  - Verify only one request/error appears

Imports:
  import { cleanupTestConfigDir, expect, launchExtensionContext, startMcpServer, test, writeTestConfig } from './fixtures.js';
  import { openSidePanel, waitForExtensionConnected } from './helpers.js';
  (Note: no startTestServer or openTestAppTab needed — the browser tools card is visible without a matching plugin tab)

Constraints:
- Do NOT try to test the actual npm install step — it requires internet access and a real package version
- Do NOT test that process.stdout.isTTY message is printed — that is a server-side stdout concern, not observable from Playwright
- Each test MUST have its own configDir (mkdtemp), server, and context to maintain full isolation
- All asserts on state that depends on WebSocket message propagation MUST use expect.poll() or toBeVisible with timeout — not plain toBe/toEqual without polling
